title: Customer charged twice for the same invoice
symptom: duplicate charge on credit card statement, same amount, same date
resolution: Confirm with Stripe dashboard (search by last-4 + amount + date range). Refund the duplicate via Stripe direct (NOT through our billing UI — it 422s on charges older than 24h). Email customer with both reference numbers + the refund ETA (5-7 business days). Route to billing_ops queue if the duplicate predates the 30-day refund window — finance has to issue a manual ACH credit instead.

title: Failed payment after trial conversion
symptom: subscription silently downgraded to free tier 24-48h after trial ended, no email warning, customer surprised
resolution: Customer's trial converted but the saved payment method failed (expired card, address mismatch, or insufficient funds). Look up the failed Stripe charge ID from the customer's account → Billing → Recent attempts. Ask customer to update their card via the self-serve portal. Then manually retry the charge from Stripe dashboard. Restore the subscription tier once the charge clears. If the customer can't update within 7 days, escalate to billing_ops — they have a workflow for invoice-based payment as a fallback.

title: SSO setup loops at the IdP login page
symptom: user redirected to IdP (Okta/Azure AD/Google Workspace), signs in successfully, redirected back to our login screen — loop repeats forever
resolution: Almost always a SAML clock-skew issue (>5 min drift between our IdP-config NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter timestamps and the customer's IdP clock). Have customer's ops team sync the NTP servers, OR widen the assertion validity window in the SAML config (we accept ±10min by default; can push to ±15min for chronic offenders). Secondary cause: incorrect SP entity ID. Verify the entity ID in our admin console exactly matches what the customer's IdP is sending (case-sensitive).

title: Locked out after password reset email
symptom: user clicks reset link from email, gets 'link expired' error
resolution: Reset links are valid for 24h from issue, single-use. Generate a fresh one from the admin console (Users → search → Send reset link). If failure repeats, check the customer's email security gateway — Mimecast, Proofpoint, and Microsoft Defender for Office 365 all pre-click links to scan them, which burns the single-use token before the actual user can click. Workaround: have the customer add our reset-email sender domain to their gateway's allowlist, OR issue them a temporary password via admin console (force-rotate at next login).

title: Export job hangs at 95% for large datasets
symptom: CSV/JSON export of >100k rows never completes, progress bar stuck at 95% indefinitely, job eventually shows 'failed' after 30min timeout
resolution: Known issue with the streaming export's chunk-flush threshold — the last 5% chunk exceeds our 10MB write buffer for wide schemas (>40 columns). Workaround: have customer filter to <50k rows per export, OR switch to the paginated API (it streams in 10k-row chunks server-side). Fix is on the v2.4 roadmap (linked ticket: ENG-4521). Bump priority if customer is enterprise-tier — they have a contractual export SLA.

title: Webhook receiver returns 200 but events never arrive
symptom: customer's webhook endpoint responds 200 OK in their access logs, but events never appear in their downstream system
resolution: Check the webhook signature header (X-MDK-Signature). If customer's HMAC secret rotated and they didn't update both sides simultaneously, our retry queue will silently drop the event after 5 consecutive signature-mismatch failures (anti-replay defense). Have them rotate the secret, update both sides in the same change window, then trigger a webhook replay from the admin panel (Settings → Webhooks → Replay last 24h). If signature is fine, check their endpoint's processing — they may return 200 BEFORE actually persisting the event, then crash silently in their queue worker.

title: Bulk import users from CSV
symptom: customer wants to onboard 100+ users at once, doesn't want to invite one-at-a-time
resolution: Use the Bulk Import wizard (Admin → Users → Bulk import). Required CSV columns: email, first_name, last_name, role. Optional: team, manager_email, invite_immediately (bool, default true). Max 5000 rows per upload. Common gotcha: the 'role' column must match EXACTLY one of our defined role slugs (case-sensitive: 'admin', 'member', 'viewer' — not 'Admin'). Validate the CSV with the dry-run toggle before committing — it'll surface row-level errors without creating any accounts.

title: Configure email-based 2FA
symptom: customer wants to require 2FA but doesn't want to mandate TOTP apps (their workforce includes warehouse staff without smartphones)
resolution: Email-based 2FA is supported but disabled by default (slightly weaker than TOTP). Enable via Admin → Security → 2FA → 'Email codes' toggle. Once enabled, each user can choose Email OR TOTP at next login. Codes are 6 digits, valid 5 minutes, rate-limited to 1 send per 30 seconds. Note: if customer's IdP already enforces 2FA (e.g. Okta MFA), our 2FA is redundant — recommend they leave it off to avoid double-prompt friction.

title: Custom domain support (CNAME)
symptom: enterprise customer wants to host our portal under their domain (e.g. portal.customer.com -> app.movate.io)
resolution: Custom domain support is roadmapped for v2.5 (ETA Q3). Today's workarounds: (1) use our subdomain feature (customer.movate.io) which is free for all tiers and supports custom SSL via Let's Encrypt; (2) for enterprise tier only, we can configure a CNAME with manual SSL certificate upload — this requires a signed amendment to the contract and a 5-business-day setup window. Log this as +1 on the FR-CUSTOM-DOMAIN ticket; product reviews customer requests at each release planning.

title: Annual contract renewal price increase
symptom: customer received renewal quote with 8-12% price increase, asking why
resolution: Standard contractual CPI adjustment — every annual contract includes a clause permitting up to 12% YoY increase. The actual % is set by Finance each Jan based on prior-year inflation. Talking points: (1) we absorbed cost increases in 2023, this is the first adjustment in 2 years; (2) the increase covers expanded support hours + the new dashboard module they're already using; (3) we can offer a 24-month renewal at the current rate to lock in pricing. Escalate to AE if customer threatens churn — they have a 5% retention discount available without exec approval.

title: SOC 2 Type II report request
symptom: prospect's procurement team asking for SOC 2 Type II report before signing
resolution: We have a current SOC 2 Type II (audited Q4 last year, covers 12 months of operations). Distribution requires a signed mutual NDA — send the prospect our standard NDA template (link in confluence: legal/templates/mnda.docx). Once signed by both sides, route to security@movate.io with the NDA attached; security ops will send the SOC 2 within 1 business day. Common follow-ups: penetration test summary (annual; same NDA covers it), data processing addendum (DPA) for GDPR/CCPA — pre-signed PDF lives at legal/dpa-v3.pdf.

title: Customer wants to enable IP allowlisting
symptom: enterprise customer wants to restrict our portal access to their corporate VPN IPs only
resolution: IP allowlisting is available on enterprise tier (Settings -> Security -> IP allowlist). Accepts CIDR ranges (e.g. 10.0.0.0/16) and single IPs. Up to 100 entries per tenant. Important caveats: (1) the customer's own admin must add their current IP first or they'll lock themselves out — we always require 2 entries minimum on first enable; (2) our webhook delivery IPs are documented at docs.movate.io/webhooks/source-ips — customers using outbound webhooks need to allowlist those on their side; (3) IPv6 ranges supported but limited to /64 prefixes for performance reasons.

title: Slack notifications not arriving in customer's channel
symptom: customer integrated Slack via OAuth, integration shows 'connected' but no messages appear in the target channel
resolution: Two common causes: (1) the OAuth scope didn't include channels:write — customer needs to disconnect + reconnect, granting the wider scope at the consent screen; (2) the bot wasn't invited to the target channel — Slack requires explicit invite for bots in private channels (not public). Walk customer through: type /invite @movate-bot in the target channel from Slack itself. If still failing, check the integration's last-error field in our admin (Integrations -> Slack -> Logs) — Slack returns a structured error code we can map.

title: Webhook deliveries failing with TLS handshake error
symptom: webhook delivery attempts fail with 'TLS handshake timeout' or 'certificate verify failed' in admin logs
resolution: Almost always the customer's endpoint has a misconfigured SSL certificate. Common causes: (1) self-signed cert — we require a valid CA-signed cert (Let's Encrypt is free and works); (2) incomplete certificate chain (missing intermediate CA) — verify with `openssl s_client -connect their.endpoint:443 -showcerts`; (3) expired cert — common after Let's Encrypt 90-day renewals fail silently. We do NOT support certificate pinning on our outbound webhook sender. If customer is enterprise tier and needs mTLS, escalate to platform_eng — it's available but requires manual setup.

title: API rate limit exceeded — 429 responses
symptom: customer's integration is getting HTTP 429 'Too Many Requests' responses
resolution: Our default rate limit is 100 req/min per API key (rolling window). Check the Retry-After response header — it tells the customer exactly how many seconds to wait. Customers can: (1) implement exponential backoff (recommended pattern: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 5 retries); (2) request a rate limit increase if they have a sustained legitimate need — enterprise tier gets 1000 req/min by default, mid-tier needs approval from AE; (3) batch API endpoints exist for several operations (bulk-create, bulk-update) — they consume 1 rate-limit unit per CALL, not per RECORD, which is often the right answer for high-volume integrations.

title: Dashboard slow to load for accounts with >50k records
symptom: customer reports the main dashboard takes 15-30 seconds to load; small accounts load in <2s
resolution: Known performance issue — the dashboard runs an unfiltered aggregation across the customer's full dataset. Workarounds (in order of effectiveness): (1) advise customer to use the date-range filter (defaulting to 'last 30 days' instead of 'all time') — typically drops load time 80%+; (2) enable the dashboard cache in their account settings (Settings -> Performance -> Dashboard cache) — refreshes every 15min instead of on-demand; (3) for accounts >500k records, we offer a pre-aggregation feature on enterprise tier (rollup tables computed nightly). Long-term fix is the columnar storage migration on the v3 roadmap.

title: New customer can't find the API key generation page
symptom: new admin user wants to wire up their first integration, can't locate API key UI
resolution: API keys live under Settings -> Developers -> API Keys (NOT under Settings -> Security, which is where SSO/2FA live — common confusion). Only users with the 'admin' role can generate keys; 'member' role sees the menu item disabled. Walk new admins through: (1) Settings -> Developers -> API Keys -> 'Generate new key'; (2) name it after the use case (e.g. 'zapier-prod', 'internal-etl'); (3) copy the key IMMEDIATELY — we show it once, then hash it. If they lose it, they have to revoke + regenerate.

title: Initial data import — what's the recommended path?
symptom: newly-signed customer asking how to get their existing data in
resolution: Three paths, depending on data size: (1) <10k records: use the Bulk Import wizard (CSV upload, see KB-007); (2) 10k-1M records: use the migration API (bulk-create endpoint, supports up to 100k records per request, async with progress polling); (3) >1M records: schedule a managed migration with our solutions engineering team — they have tooling for direct DB-to-DB copies from common sources (Salesforce, Hubspot, Postgres). All paths support a dry-run mode that surfaces validation errors before committing. Always recommend customers start with a sandbox tenant for their first 1-2 imports to learn the data shape.

title: Customer wants webhook signing with their own HMAC secret
symptom: customer wants to set the HMAC secret used to sign outbound webhooks (today we generate it)
resolution: Customer-supplied HMAC secrets are not supported today — we generate and rotate them on our side, customer reads via API. This is roadmapped (FR-WEBHOOK-CUSTOM-HMAC) but blocked on a security review (customer-supplied secrets need a HSM-backed storage path to meet our SOC 2 controls). ETA: v2.6, contingent on the security review landing. Workaround for customers needing custom auth: they can fetch our generated secret via /api/v1/webhooks/secrets and use it on their verification side — same outcome, just we control rotation.

title: Customer reports data loss — multiple records missing
symptom: customer reports records that existed yesterday are gone today, no record of who deleted them
resolution: P0 — page on-call immediately (PagerDuty: 'data-loss' service). DO NOT promise the data is recoverable until eng confirms — we have 7-day backups but restoration is record-level only, not bulk. While on-call investigates: (1) pull the audit log for the customer's tenant (Admin -> Audit -> filter by 'delete') — most 'data loss' is a user with admin permissions accidentally bulk-deleting; (2) check for active integrations that have delete scope — third-party tools occasionally fire delete operations during 'sync' cycles; (3) verify the customer is looking at the correct workspace (multi-workspace customers sometimes log into the wrong one). If genuine data loss confirmed, eng restores from backup; communications go through the AE + CSM, not support directly.

title: Possible security incident — customer reports unauthorized access
symptom: customer admin sees logins from IPs/locations they don't recognize, suspects credential compromise
resolution: P0 security incident — page security on-call (PagerDuty: 'security-incident' service). Immediate actions while security responds: (1) force a session revocation on the affected user (Admin -> Users -> search -> 'Revoke all sessions'); (2) reset their password and require re-auth at next login; (3) if 2FA was not enabled on the account, enable it organization-wide for the affected workspace as a precaution. Pull the auth-log for the suspicious IPs — geolocate via our internal IP intel tool. Do NOT delete any audit logs — security team needs the forensic trail. Communications: AE + customer's security contact only; never publicly acknowledge an incident.

title: Email notifications arrive 30+ minutes delayed
symptom: customer reports notification emails taking 30+ minutes to arrive (or never arriving)
resolution: Check our notification queue status first (status.movate.io -> 'Notifications'). If queue is healthy, the issue is downstream: (1) check the customer's domain reputation — emails from new tenants sometimes get greylisted by aggressive spam filters (G Suite, Office 365); we can request whitelisting from our ESP (SendGrid) — takes 2-3 business days; (2) verify the customer's SPF/DKIM records if they're using a vanity sender domain — misconfigured records cause silent drops; (3) check their email gateway's quarantine — Mimecast/Proofpoint sometimes hold our emails for 'manual review'. Last resort: the customer can use webhook notifications instead of email for time-sensitive alerts.

title: Exporting all data for GDPR right-to-access request
symptom: customer received a GDPR/CCPA data-access request from one of their end users, needs to export everything we have on that subject
resolution: Use the Subject Data Export endpoint (API only — no UI yet). Required input: the subject's email or unique identifier. Returns a ZIP within 24h via signed S3 URL containing: (1) all records mentioning the subject; (2) audit-log entries; (3) any uploaded files referencing them. Customer is responsible for delivering this to the end user. For DELETE-right requests, use the Right to Erasure endpoint — it's irreversible and we ALSO purge our backups of that subject's records after the 30-day retention window. SLA: 30 days max for the underlying GDPR/CCPA timeline, but we typically deliver in <24h.

title: Customer needs invoice in their local currency
symptom: customer in EU/UK wants invoices in EUR/GBP instead of USD
resolution: We can issue invoices in EUR, GBP, AUD, or CAD in addition to USD (Stripe limits us to these five). To switch: customer's billing admin opens a request via Settings -> Billing -> Currency change. Finance reviews + approves within 2 business days. The exchange rate locks at the time of approval and applies to all subsequent invoices until a future change. Note: switching currencies prorates the current billing period. We do NOT support currency-per-invoice — it's a tenant-wide setting. Tax handling differs by currency: USD invoices show tax on US states where we have nexus; EUR shows reverse-charge VAT for B2B EU customers.

title: Saved filter views disappearing on refresh
symptom: customer creates a saved filter (e.g. 'High-priority tickets'), reloads the page, the filter is gone
resolution: Known bug for users with browser ad-blockers / privacy extensions (uBlock Origin, Privacy Badger, Ghostery). Our filter state persists via a third-party analytics SDK that some blockers categorize as 'tracking' — when blocked, the localStorage write silently fails. Workaround: (1) advise customer to allowlist app.movate.io in their blocker; (2) we have a fix in progress (ENG-7891) to move filter state to our own /api/v1/views endpoint instead of the SDK — ETA Q3. For now, customers can use the URL-based filter (the query string preserves filter state across refreshes even when the SDK is blocked).

title: Setting up alerts for budget thresholds
symptom: customer wants to get notified when their monthly usage approaches their plan limit
resolution: Budget alerts live under Settings -> Billing -> Usage Alerts. Customers can set thresholds at 50%, 75%, 90%, 100% of plan limits (multi-select). Alerts fire to: (1) the billing admin's email (default); (2) optionally Slack/Teams via integration; (3) optionally a webhook. Alert calculation refreshes every 15 minutes (NOT real-time) — heavy bursts can briefly exceed plan limits before the alert fires. Once an alert fires, we DON'T auto-throttle — customers must choose to upgrade their plan, or accept overage charges (configured per-customer in their contract). For hard caps (no overage), customers need to contact billing@movate.io to set up a usage ceiling.

title: Zapier integration loses authentication after a few days
symptom: customer's Zapier zaps stop firing every few days, requiring them to reconnect
resolution: Two possible causes: (1) the API key the customer used in Zapier has an expiration set (default 90 days for new keys) — check via Settings -> Developers -> API Keys -> look at expiry column; if expiring frequently, regenerate without an expiry (enterprise tier only) or extend to the max (1 year for non-enterprise); (2) Zapier's OAuth integration occasionally drops auth after Zapier deploys — they auto-recover within 24h; if longer, customer reconnects the Zapier app from the integrations page. Long-term: recommend customers use Zapier's API-key auth path (more stable than OAuth) for production zaps.

title: Customer wants nested workspaces / org hierarchy
symptom: enterprise customer (parent company + subsidiaries) wants to manage subsidiaries as nested workspaces under one parent
resolution: Nested workspaces are NOT supported today — each workspace is a flat tenant with its own billing, users, data. Workarounds for hierarchy-needing customers: (1) use the multi-workspace feature (enterprise tier only) — one user identity can be a member of multiple workspaces with SSO; usage aggregates roll up in the admin portal; (2) use API-level prefixes/tags to model their hierarchy within a single workspace — works for read-only reporting, not for permission boundaries. True nested workspaces are roadmapped (FR-NESTED-ORGS) but blocked on a billing-engine refactor — no ETA. Always log enterprise interest on the FR — it's a top-5 enterprise ask.

title: Configuring per-user MFA enforcement
symptom: customer wants to require 2FA for admin users but not regular members
resolution: Granular MFA enforcement is supported on Pro and Enterprise tiers (Settings -> Security -> MFA -> 'Per-role enforcement'). Available levels: (1) 'optional for all' — default; (2) 'required for admins'; (3) 'required for all users'. The setting takes effect at the user's next login — currently-active sessions stay valid. Combine with the 'session timeout' setting (24h default) to force re-auth + MFA enrollment. If using SSO, MFA enforcement applies on top of the IdP's own policies — users prompted for both. For customers preferring single-MFA, configure the IdP MFA only and set our enforcement to 'optional'.

title: Customer threatening churn over feature gap
symptom: AE flags account at risk; customer cites missing feature(s) as primary churn reason
resolution: Not a support issue per se — route to the AE + CS team immediately, NOT into the standard FR queue. Talking points support can offer while waiting for AE: (1) acknowledge the gap explicitly, don't oversell roadmap; (2) ask whether a workaround would meet their need short-term (often yes for read-only reporting gaps); (3) offer to set up a roadmap review call with PM. Internal next steps: (a) check the FR ticket — is this on the v2.5/v2.6 roadmap? If yes, share ETA; (b) if not on any roadmap, escalate to product leadership — they have a 'churn-saver' weekly triage for feature gaps blocking >$50k ARR. Document the conversation in the customer's CRM record with tags [churn-risk, feature-gap].
